title: Satan's Saint
description: Satan's Saint is a autobiography by Guy Endore. Restricted 18 in 1968.
descriptionParagraphs: Satan's Saint is a autobiography by Guy Endore. Restricted 18 in 1968.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a autobiography, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Restricted 18 in 1968. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Satan's Saint is a autobiography by Guy Endore. Restricted 18 in 1968.
banningParagraphs: Satan's Saint entered censorship debates as a autobiography associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.
The earliest event currently captured here is 1968 in New Zealand, where Indecent Publications Tribunal classified, prohibited, or restricted. Restricted 18 in 1968. Restricted 18 in 1968.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.
